sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what changed! When I pressed UP:
- Row 38: `gggggg` (gray) became `ggNNgg` (gray-green inside) - the green block moved UP
- Row 39: `gggggg` became `ggNNgg` (green inside)
- Row 40: `ggNNgg` (green inside) became `gggggg` (gray)
- Row 41: `ggNNgg` (green inside) became `gggggg` (gray)
- Row 63: `WWWW...` became `GWWWW...` - a progress bar at the bottom moved!
So pressing UP moved the green block (N) UP. The green block (N) inside the gray frame moved UP by 2 rows. And the white bar at the bottom (row 63 moved one cell to the right - it's now `GWWWW...` instead of `W` at the start.
So the game mechanic is:
- There's a player cursor/selector that moves around the left panel, and pressing UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the selected piece. The white bar at the bottom is a progress bar.
Wait, actually looking more carefully, the green block (N) inside the gray frame moved UP. But which piece was selected? Let me think...
Looking at the initial state, the green block (N) was at rows 38-41 (inside the gray frame at rows 39-42). After pressing UP, the green moved to rows 38-39 (inside the gray at rows 38-41). So the green block moved up by 2 rows.
But wait - the gray frame also moved. The gray frame was at rows 39-42, and now it's at rows 38-41. So the entire gray+green object moved up by 2 rows.
The white bar at the bottom moved one cell to the right - it's now `G instead of `W` at the start.
So the game mechanic seems like a step counter or progress bar.
Let me try pressing UP again to see what happens. Let me try pressing DOWN to see if the piece moves down.
